what_it_does: Records a design decision rejecting a radial menu / command-center UI for BD-G and prescribing flat, thumb-reachable controls instead.
observations: YAML frontmatter identifies the item as a current block sourced from claude_code in the dragnet-development channel.
Title uses 'P10 · ❌ The radial menu / command center (BD-G)'.
Body argues radial menus are a design flourish that is hard to execute well on phones and easy to do badly.
Proposes decomposing the radial menu functions into flat controls: submit button at bottom, replies badge in top bar, doc switcher in hamburger.
References a request from Tim for 'the V symbol that opens stuff' and reframes the underlying need as 'reach my session controls'.
Recommends building flat controls now and considering a V-overlay only if flat controls feel cluttered.
standouts: Explicit rejection of a radial command-center UI (❌ in title).
Mobile-first usability rationale against radial geometry.
Named stakeholder context: Tim's V-symbol request.
Pragmatic 'build flat first, consider overlay later' guidance.
conventions: Uses noticeboard item YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Markdown body with a level-3 heading mirroring the title.
Uses board:// URI for item addressing and part_of linking.
Labels design items with P-number prefix and status emoji.
concerns: Design claim is stated as fact without cited user research or data.
Reference to 'Tim' lacks role/context for readers outside the session.
Recommendation is firm but conditional, which could be read differently by implementers.
notes: A noticeboard design note that rejects a radial command-center UI for BD-G and prescribes simple, thumb-reachable flat controls, with a conditional future overlay option.
questions: What does BD-G refer to in this project?
What is the parent item board://item-f9f4a6a6?
Who is Tim and what is his role in the design decision?
What specific phone/platform constraints informed the mobile usability judgment?
What does the existing or planned flat control layout look like?
purpose_vs_actual: The title names the rejected concept; the body is a decision record that rejects it and proposes an alternative — aligned as a design rationale document.
intention_signals: To capture and communicate a design decision that pushes back against a radial menu / V-symbol command center, redirecting the team toward flat, reachable controls while preserving the reasoning and stakeholder context.
novelty: Explicitly documents a rejected UI pattern together with the named stakeholder request it responds to and a concrete alternative breakdown.
contribution: Preserves the BD-G session-controls design rationale, the rejection of radial menus, the flat-control alternative decomposition, and the stakeholder context around Tim's V-symbol request so a fused system can retain this decision and its reasoning.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard decision record titled 'P10 · ❌ The radial menu / command center (BD-G)' that rejects a radial command-center UI for mobile in favor of flat, thumb-reachable controls and documents the rationale.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate a design decision to avoid a radial menu for BD-G and instead build simple flat session controls, with a conditional future V-overlay option.
